prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for aniline were computed with the chemkit fukui-reactivity skill at the DFT tier `standard` level — that is B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density fitting — in PySCF.  

Method provenance block
• Property: atom-condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor, Mulliken scheme  
• Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` = B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fitted)  
• Geometry provenance: supplied fixed geometry from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz; no re-optimization (single-points for N, N+1, and N–1 states)  
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)  
• Charge and multiplicity: neutral singlet (0,1); cation doublet ( +1,2 ); anion doublet ( –1,2 )  
• Software: chemkit / PySCF backend  
• Convergence & warnings: SCF cycles reported converged for all three charge states; see warnings below.  

Headline result (most electrophilic site)  
C(3) f⁺ = 2.2975867 (unitless)  

The live engine log was written during the run to the caller’s working directory (default <skill>_timestamp.out); the full result JSON is at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp_jw_lmrw/live.json as shown in the CLI invocation, and contains the full f⁺/f⁻/f⁰ arrays and charges.

Warnings returned by the tool (verbatim):
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)  
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)  
• common header present
